description: Merged Triangle Assessment Set. Combines original tension-heavy scenarios with purified vertices for recognition/achievement and new AI-era behavioral trade-offs. Targets ~2 measurements per dimension across Gain and Loss frames.

triangles.title: The Ideal Offer
triangles.prompt: You have three job offers, each perfect in only one way. Drag the ball to the compromise you'd actually make.

a.label: Top-tier salary, but the work is stressful and constant

b.label: Complete flexibility and peace of mind, but the pay is just 'okay'

c.label: A deep sense of community and shared values, but the hours are long

triangles.title: The Nature of the Work
triangles.prompt: If you could design your daily workflow, which pull is strongest?

a.label: Total independence: you decide the 'what' and the 'how'

b.label: Absolute stability: you know exactly what is expected and your future is secure

c.label: Pure creativity: the chance to build something original and expressive

triangles.title: When the Fog Rolls In
triangles.prompt: You are leading a project where the tools and goals change every week. What keeps you moving?

a.label: The 'high' of conquering a problem that seems impossible to others

b.label: The ability to stay calm and effective even when you don't have all the facts

c.label: The speed at which you can learn new systems and pivot your approach

triangles.title: The Energy Drain
triangles.prompt: Which of these environments would burn you out the fastest? Drag the ball AWAY from that corner (or toward the things you'd tolerate more).

a.label: Working in total isolation with no human connection

b.label: Working on tasks that have zero impact on the real world

c.label: Working in a rigid system where you have zero creative input

triangles.title: The Dealbreaker
triangles.prompt: A company is recruiting you. Which of these 'missing' elements is the ultimate dealbreaker?

a.label: No room for growth: you'll be doing the same thing in 5 years

b.label: No skin in the game: your performance doesn't affect your rewards

c.label: No collaboration: it's every person for themselves

triangles.title: The Future Self
triangles.prompt: Ten years from now, you are 'successful.' What is the core of that success?

a.label: You are the recognized authority that everyone in the industry knows by name

b.label: You have mastered a craft to a level of depth that few humans reach

c.label: You are the 'glue'—the person who builds the teams that change the world

triangles.title: Conquest vs. Status
triangles.prompt: If you could only protect one aspect of your reputation in a high-stakes project, which is it?

a.label: The internal win: Knowing you conquered a challenge at the edge of your ability

b.label: The external win: Being seen as the definitive expert and leader in the room

c.label: The steady win: Knowing your work provides a predictable foundation for your future

triangles.title: The Orchestrator vs. The Maker
triangles.prompt: A new tool allows you to produce 10x more work. Where do you find your 'flow' in this new world?

a.label: Directing a system of agents and tools to achieve a massive result

b.label: Doing the core creative work yourself because you love the process

c.label: Rapid iteration: trying 50 small experiments to see what works

triangles.title: When the Plan Fails
triangles.prompt: A major project is suddenly cancelled. What is your immediate, natural response?

a.label: You immediately start looking for the next wave to ride, unbothered by the loss

b.label: You stay calm and start methodically organizing the chaos into a new structure

c.label: You focus on the team, ensuring everyone feels supported during the transition
